name: TMA back/close action on invitation accept deep-link entry
description: Fix a TMA bug where the Telegram BackButton (and any system back action wired to it) on the AcceptInvitationPage was a no-op. The page is reached exclusively via a `?startapp=<token>` deep link, so the React Router history contains only the redirect entry. RootLayout's `navigate(-1)` had nothing to navigate to and the user remained stuck on the page. Move BackButton ownership to the page itself so the back/close action calls `miniApp.close()` and exits the mini app, matching the deep-link 'go back to Telegram' expectation. Bumps TMA to 1.0.2.

planned: Detect the invitation accept route in RootLayout and skip the central BackButton binding on that path.
Have AcceptInvitationPage mount its own BackButton that closes the mini app on click, with the standard light-impact haptic.
Bump apps/tma/package.json to 1.0.2 so the on-screen app version label reflects the fix.
artifacts: `apps/tma/src/app/router/root-layout.tsx` — added `isInvitationAcceptRoute` memo (matches `TMA_PATHS.invitations` and `${TMA_PATHS.invitations}/...`); the BackButton effect returns early on that path so the page can own the click binding.

`apps/tma/src/features/invitations/pages/accept-invitation-page.tsx` — new effect that mounts `backButton.onClick(() => { impact('light'); miniApp.close.ifAvailable() })` with `backButton.show()` on mount and `backButton.hide()` on cleanup, gated on `backButton.isSupported()`. Imports `backButton` and `miniApp` from `@tma.js/sdk` and adds `impact` to the existing haptics import.
verification: `pnpm --filter tma build` succeeded (manual confirmation of `AcceptInvitationPage` chunk still resolves; new import of `backButton`/`miniApp` is already tree-shaken into the page bundle).

notes: Per `docs/references/frontend/tma/native-ui-and-navigation-pattern.md`, root routes with no meaningful back target should hide or own the BackButton. The acceptance page is a deep-link entry with no in-app history, so the page now owns BackButton and the back action closes the mini app. TMA vertical-swipe close is intentionally disabled in `telegram-init.ts` for scrollable surfaces; that is unaffected by this fix and the user-facing close path is the BackButton / Android system back mapped to it. The on-screen app version label (rendered from `VITE_APP_VERSION`) will now show 1.0.2 on the home, 404, and fatal-launch screens.
